gold_change_reason: Seed solution only handled single tumor with transposition for multi-tumor; scaled task requires multi-tumor without transposition and placeholder handling.
covered_requirements: Increase data complexity by adding a second tumor to the input line, requiring the parser to handle multi-tumor formatting (transposed copy numbers and probabilities).
Introduce alphabetic placeholders in the C and p* columns to test robust handling of non-numeric values (None conversion).
implementation_notes: For multi-tumor, C and p* are parsed by splitting on colon and then each part by comma, without transposition, to match expected output.
Placeholders (non-numeric strings) are converted to None using try/except.
Single-tumor case retains original behavior: C split by colon into one flat list, p* split by comma into a flat list.
assumptions: In single-tumor inputs, C uses colon-separated values and p* uses comma-separated values.
In multi-tumor inputs, both C and p* use colon-separated groups, each containing comma-separated values.
